text: Planning a trip can be as exciting as the journey itself. I spent weeks researching my destination, from its history to local customs. When I finally arrived, everything felt new and vibrant. I tried street food that I couldn’t pronounce, like a spicy noodle dish with a name I still forget. Language barriers were tricky, but smiles and gestures worked wonders. Exploring new cultures taught me to appreciate differences, like how people greet each other or celebrate holidays. Now, those memories—tasting unknown flavors, getting lost in charming alleys—stay with me. Travel isn’t just about seeing places; it’s about collecting stories that last a lifetime.
